name: Veal Scallopine with Marsala
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Scallopine of veal, cut into 1/4 inch slices
recipeInstructions: Unless the scallopine are very small, cut them into pieces measuring approximately 3 inches by 3 inches or slightly larger. Place them between sheets of wax paper and pound lightly to flatten (use a rolling pin, bottom of a heavy skillet, pounder, etc.). Blend flour with salt and pepper to taste. Dip the meat into flour to coat lightly. Using a large, heavy skillet, heat the butter and oil until very hot but not brown, then add the meat in one layer. Cook over relatively high heat until golden brown on one side. Turn the pieces and cook until golden brown on the other. The cooking time should be from 4-6 minutes. Transfer the meat to a serving platter or two plates and keep warm. To the skillet, add the wine and stir to dissolve the particles in the pan. Reduce the wine slightly and pour equal amounts over each serving. Naturally, these amounts may be increased to serve more people. For Chicken Marsala, I don't see why you can't substitute chicken breasts.
